Article Title: Voter ID row: Third non-bailable warrant issued against actor Prakash Raj
Article Text:
Actor Prakash Raj repeatedly failed to appear in a case alleging that his name appeared on multiple electoral rolls across 4 states

NEW DELHI: A third non-bailable warrant was issued against Actor Prakash Raj after he failed to appear before a Bengaluru court in a case alleging that his name figured in four electoral rolls spread across three states. The warrant was reissued on June 12 on a petition filed by advocate K Dilip Kumar, with the next hearing set for July 25.The latest order intensifies the legal trouble for the actor, who has remained absent despite earlier summons and two previous non-bailable warrants issued by the court.Court records show that a summons was first issued on February 17, through the Commissioner of Police. However, authorities reported that Raj could not be located at the address mentioned in the petition.After he failed to appear, the court issued its first non-bailable warrant on March 17, noting: "It is reported that the accused has vacated the house. The accused called out absent. Issue NBW to the accused."When the matter came up again on April 17 and Raj did not appear, the warrant was reissued. The court issued the third NBW on June 12, with the proceedings recording that the presiding officer had been away on training.At the heart of the case are allegations that Raj's name appeared in multiple voter lists while he was contesting the 2019 Lok Sabha election from Bengaluru Central.In his complaint, advocate K Dilip Kumar alleged that besides being enrolled as a voter in Bengaluru's Shantinagar Assembly constituency, Raj's name also appeared at two locations in Chennai's Velachery Assembly constituency in Tamil Nadu and in Telangana's Serilingampally Assembly constituency.The complainant said he had approached the local police in April 2019 seeking action, but alleged that no steps were taken. He subsequently moved the court, which took up the matter in 2023.The case gathered momentum on August 1, 2025, when the court said it had taken cognisance "for the offences punishable under Sections 31 and 125(A) of Representation of the People Act, 1950." It then directed that summons be issued to the actor.Since then, the proceedings have steadily escalated, moving from summons to repeated non-bailable warrants as Raj allegedly continued to stay away from court, setting the stage for a crucial hearing next month.
Article Summary: Actor Prakash Raj repeatedly failed to appear in a case alleging that his name appeared on multiple electoral rolls across 4 states. The latest order intensifies the legal trouble for the actor, who has remained absent despite earlier summons and two previous non-bailable warrants issued by the court.

Article Title: Congress slams Madhya Pradesh CM over land deals, Akhilesh sees 'BJP plot' to topple 3 CMs
Article Text:
Madhya Pradesh CM Mohan Yadav

LUCKNOW/NEW DELHI: Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav alleged on Tuesday that land-related accusations against Madhya Pradesh CM Mohan Yadav were part of a BJP plot to discredit and replace 3 of its CMs, including in Rajasthan and UP, even as Congress attacked the MP CM The allegation followed a report by ‘The Indian Express’ that Mohan Yadav’s family and real estate firms had acquired at least 137 plots spanning 168 acres in Ujjain for Rs 45 crore in 2years since Dec 2023. The plots were allegedly concentrated in areas that benefited from road projects and land-use changes announced by MP govt.“If those are the allegations against Mohan Yadav, UP CM has bought 300-600 acres of land,” Akhilesh alleged in Lucknow. Congress also trained its guns on Mohan Yadav, alleging ‘plunder’.Under BJP’s ‘double-engine’ government in Madhya Pradesh, ‘engine of plunder’ is running at full speed,” Congress general secretary Jairam Ramesh said in a Hindi post on X, sharing the news report.MP chief minister Mohan Yadav himself was at helm of this alleged “looting”, Ramesh alleged. He also pointed to speculation that the Union agriculture minister, who moved from MP to Centre, had orchestrated “planting of stories” against the chief minister — an apparent reference to Shivraj Singh Chouhan.“The infighting appears to be over seat of power and a share in spoils,” Ramesh said.ALSO READ: 'Engine of loot': Jairam Ramesh cites media report, alleges MP CM Mohan Yadav involved in corruption Akhilesh Yadav rejected allegations against Mohan Yadav, saying land purchases by his family were not new because he had been in real estate before becoming chief minister. “Wasn’t BJP aware that he was into real estate business? You know why these allegations are being levelled at this stage? This is because BJP is looking for ways to replace 3 CMs,” Akhilesh said.He alleged BJP was working to remove Mohan Yadav and the CMs of Rajasthan and UP. But a change of guard would not alter BJP’s fortunes in UP, he said, predicting SP’s “bicycle” would race past rivals in 2027 assembly polls.“In upcoming elections, Samajwadi Party’s cycle will move at a speed that all other parties will clear its way on their own,” he said, drawing cheers from party workers.Congress general secretary KC Venugopal questioned why central investigative agencies had not acted against Mohan Yadav. “PM Modi, why have ED-CBI not knocked on doors of MP CM Mohan Yadav? Is a 253-acre mega land scam not grounds enough to chase him and punish him for his crimes?” Venugopal asked.He alleged BJP used investigative agencies to hound opposition politicians, settle political scores and pressure rivals into joining its alliance, but had gone silent when its own functionaries were accused of looting public wealth.
Article Summary: Akhilesh Yadav says land-related accusations against Mohan Yadav are part of a BJP plot to discredit and replace 3 of its CMs, including in Rajasthan and UP. The allegation followed a report by ‘The Indian Express’ that Yadav’s family and real estate firms had acquired at least 137 plots spanning 168 acres in Ujjain for Rs 45 crore.

Article Title: Marathwada MLC polls deliver expected wins to Mahayuti allies
Article Text:
BJP workers celebrating victory in the MLC poll near the vote counting centre in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar:





The Marathwada local authorities’ elections largely followed expected lines, with BJP and Shiv Sena winning three and one seats, respectively, but suspected cross-voting from the MVA camp significantly boosted Mahayuti’s victory margins in three of the four constituencies.In the Aurangabad-Jalna constituency, BJP’s Suhas Shirsath secured a decisive win over Shiv Sena (UBT) candidate Ganesh Lokhande, polling 455 votes — reportedly exceeding the NDA ’s estimated strength — and winning by a margin of 320 votes.“I was confident of getting more than 450 votes. I would work for the cause of people and strengthen my party further,” said Shirsath, a close associate of Rajasthan Governor Haribhau Bagade.A similar pattern played out in Nanded, where BJP’s sitting MLC Amar Rajurkar defeated MVA-backed Krushna Patil by 255 votes amid claims of cross-voting in favour of Mahayuti. The contest had drawn attention against the backdrop of ‘Operation Tiger’, with Patil being the son of Sena (UBT) MP Nagesh Patil Ashtikar.The most striking result came from Osmanabad–Latur–Beed, where BJP’s Basavraj Patil registered a record 719-vote victory margin, the highest in Maharashtra.The landslide is widely attributed to large-scale cross-voting, reportedly influenced by rebel Sena (UBT) MP Omprakash Rajenimbalkar and others.In contrast, the Parbhani–Hingoli seat saw a relatively close contest, with Shiv Sena’s Saeed Khan defeating Sena (UBT) nominee Vivek Navandar by just 83 votes. Here, suspected cross-voting from the NCP camp is believed to have aided MVA, limiting the winning margin.Despite the setbacks, MVA politicians framed the contest as a principled fight rather than a numbers game. “We knew we did not have the magic figure of voters with us. Still, our fight was for democracy and to protect its democratic principles,” said Congress general secretary Jitendra Dehade.
Article Summary: The Marathwada local authorities’ elections largely followed expected lines, with BJP and Shiv Sena winning three and one seats, respectively. But suspected cross-voting from the MVA camp significantly boosted Mahayuti’s victory margins in three of the four constituencies.

Article Title: ​​Rebel Sena (UBT) MP’s son loses Nanded MLC poll, suspended from party
Article Text:
Krushna Patil

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar:





MVA-backed candidate Krushna Patil, who is the son of rebel Hingoli Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Nagesh Patil Ashitikar, lost the Nanded local authorities’ constituency poll to BJP ’s Amar Rajurkar by 255 votes. Soon after the results were announced, Sena (UBT) MP Sanjay Raut announced that Krushna Patil has been expelled for his anti-party activities.Patil secured only 84 votes while Rajurkar garnered 339 votes.The MLC poll for Nanded was held under the shadow of “Operation Tiger” that eventually, on Monday, saw the Hingoli Sena (UBT) MP leaving the Uddhav Thackeray faction to join Shiv Sena-led by deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde.Speaking to the reporters after the defeat, Krsuhna Patil said he would not divulge his future political path at present. He also said that Sena (UBT) had set irrational expectations for him to prove his loyalty to MVA.He was referring to comments by Sena (UBT) deputy leader Sushama Andhare, who had asked Patil to perform symbolic last rites of his father and also organise ‘jode maro’ (hit with shoes) agitation against Ashtikar to prove his loyalty. Andhare’s comments had sparked a war of words with Patil terming her “mentally imbalanced”.“Now, I will offer a befitting welcome to my father (after his return from Mumbai). Entire Maharashtra should come to Nanded airport to witness it,” Patil said.Congress was keen to contest the MLC polls in Nanded with its candidate Ramdas Patil filing the nomination. He, however, was asked to withdraw his nomination to make way for Patil.Notably, while Sena (UBT) had virtually disowned Patil in the wake of his father rebelling against Thackeray, the local unit of Congress had issued a whip asking its voters to vote for the MVA candidate.
Article Summary: MVA-backed candidate Krushna Patil lost the Nanded local authorities’ constituency poll to BJP’s Amar Rajurkar by 255 votes. Patil is the son of rebel Hingoli Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Nagesh Patil Ashitikar. The MLC poll for Nanded was held under the shadow of ‘Operation Tiger’ that eventually saw Patil leave the Uddhav Thackeray faction.

Article Title: KMC demolishes unauthorized encroachments in Tawde Hotel area
Article Text:
Kolhapur Municipal Corporation on Monday morning began a drive to raze 13 commercial constructions in a flood zone on the Tawade hotel to Nigadewadi road.

Kolhapur:





The civic body on Monday started a drive to raze 13 commercial constructions in a flood zone on the Tawde hotel to Nigadewadi road.The action was part of the first phase of a planned crackdown on 204 illegal constructions in the area. Of the total encroachments, 177 encroachers filed a reconsideration petition in the court again.As decision to their petitions is likely to be taken on June 25, the Kolhapur Municipal Corporation (KMC) initiated action against the remaining 27 unauthorised constructions in the first phase, which did not go to court with a writ petition.Officials said that of the 204 illegal constructions, 37 constructions were in the blue line, while 88 were in contempt of court.Ramesh Maskar, KMC’s city engineer, said, “The Supreme Court , after hearing the case between Uchgaon Gram Panchayat and the KMC, gave orders that the illegal constructions made in Tawde Hotel and Gandhi Nagar areas fall under the jurisdiction of the KMC. Notices were issued to these constructions under the Maharashtra Resilience Development Program (MRDP). A stay has been received by the encroachers who have filed a writ-petition with the Bombay high court’s circuit bench in Kolhapur.Accordingly, action has been initiated against the remaining constructions.”The civic body deployed three teams and a heavy police force for the demolition drive. An assistant commissioner, a deputy city engineer, engineers, a surveyor, 30 civic employees, three earth moving machinery, six dumpers, two gas-cutters, a tractor trolley, among other other staff (totaling around 600 personnel) were part of Monday’s demolition drive.KMC commissioner Dr Rajendra Bharud said, “A total of 13 encroachments made in the blue line adjacent to the flyover at Tawde Hotel area were razed on Monday. All these properties were commercial in nature. On residential properties, the decision to raze them will be made on June 25 after the high court’s verdict.”He added, “From Saturday, our action against these encroachments will be resumed. All the citizens are requested to obtain permission from the civic town planning department before any construction. If anyone violates the rules and carries out constructions in no construction zone, then the civic body will take necessary action.”
Article Summary: The action was part of the first phase of a planned crackdown on 204 illegal constructions in the area. Of the total encroachments, 177 encroachers filed a reconsideration petition in the court again. The decision to raze residential properties will be made on June 25 after the high court’s verdict.

Article Title: Teen who rammed SUV into woman before crashing into 6 vehicles in Navi Mumbai booked for drunk driving
Article Text:
Drunk teen SUV driver drags woman 50m, hits 6 parked vehicles

Watch CCTV Captures Delhi Teen’s Speeding SUV Seconds Before Fatal Dwarka Crash CCTV Captures Delhi Teen’s Speeding SUV Seconds Before Fatal Dwarka Crash

NAVI MUMBAI: The CBD-Belapur police have invoked section 185 of the Motor Vehicle Act for drunk driving against the accused 19-year-old SUV driver.The teenager had hit and dragged a woman pedestrian for 50 metres and caused her severe injuries and then rammed into six vehicles parked on the roadside in Artist village, sector-8 on Friday.Arun Pawar, senior inspector of CBD-Belapur said, the accused SUV driver Vikram Kamble's medical test report received from NMMC hospital, Nerul confirmed that he was under the influence of alcohol while driving.Subsequently, relevant section 185 Of Motor Vehicle Act that penalizes drunk driving were invoked against him and will be mentioned in the chargesheet that will be submitted in the court.The woman Mohini Shinde (35), who worked as househelp, sustained severe head injuries and bruises on her face and near the knees is recuperating at MGM hospital, CBD-Belapur and is out of danger, informed doctors.The accident took place in the residential locality of Artist village in Sector-8, CBD-Belapur around 5.30 pm on Friday. Police said the speeding SUV hit the woman from behind while she was walking close to the pavement and dragged her for nearly 50 metres before crashing into six vehicles parked along the roadside.Local residents rushed the injured woman to MGM Hospital, where she received prompt treatment. The incident was also captured on CCTV cameras installed in the area.Police said the SUV driver Vikram Gangadhar Kamble (19) was accompanied by his relative Akash Chandrakant Kamble (26), a private chauffeur. Both were detained by locals at the spot before the police arrived, following which an FIR was registered for rash and negligent driving and damage to property.
Article Summary: The CBD-Belapur police have invoked section 185 of the Motor Vehicle Act for drunk driving against the accused 19-year-old SUV driver. The teenager had hit and dragged a woman pedestrian for 50 metres and caused her severe injuries and then rammed into six vehicles parked on the roadside. The woman Mohini Shinde (35), who worked as househelp, sustained severe head injuries and bruises on her face and near the knees.

Article Title: 16-year-old girl gang-raped by two men aged over 60 near Mumbai
Article Text:
(The victim's identity has not been revealed to protect her privacy as per Supreme court directives on cases related to sexual assault)





THANE: A 16-year-old girl was allegedly raped by two senior citizens in Maharashtra's Thane district, police said on Sunday.The accused, both above 60 years of age, were arrested in connection with the attack, which occurred in a slum colony in Netivali village on Saturday night.According to police, the duo allegedly caught hold of the girl and raped her repeatedly.The survivor managed to escape their clutches and lodged a complaint with the police.The accused have been booked under the relevant provisions of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) Act.
Article Summary: A 16-year-old girl was allegedly raped by two senior citizens in Maharashtra's Thane district. The accused, both above 60 years of age, were arrested in connection with the attack. The survivor managed to escape their clutches and lodged a complaint with the police.

Article Title: Nashik MLC polls winner Gokul Gite joins Shiv Sena as associate member
Article Text:
Gite secured 357 votes in the election, defeating Darade by 109 votes and comfortably crossing the winning quota of 303.

Nashik

:

TOI





Hours after defeating Shiv Sena ’s official candidate Narendra Darade in the Nashik local authorities election, independent candidate Gokul Gite joined Shiv Sena as an associate member in the legislative council.The swift political move raised eyebrows among Shiv Sena and BJP politicians, while Darade claimed that deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde was well aware of how the party’s candidate was treated by BJP during the contest.“Even before the election results were officially declared, a close associate of Gokul Gite received a call from a Shiv Sena minister asking him to come to Mumbai by a chartered flight,” said a Shiv Sena member backing Darade. “Though the minister initially denied it, claiming such a quick move was impossible, he eventually flew to Nashik himself to bring Gite to Mumbai by plane. It strongly suggests that the entire sequence of events was pre-planned,” he added.A BJP politician also expressed surprise at the development. “On June 9, three ministers — Girish Mahajan, Uday Samant and Dada Bhuse — were persuading Gite to pull out of the race.Suddenly, after the results are out, MLC Gokul Gite, a close confidante of Girish Mahajan, is in Shiv Sena. This surprises everybody,” he said.Gite secured 357 votes in the election, defeating Darade by 109 votes and comfortably crossing the winning quota of 303.Darade, however, appeared to play it safe when asked if he shared these suspicions. “We were ready to contest the election straight forward manner, but were told it would go against the interests of Mahayuti. We were betrayed, and Shiv Sena chief and deputy CM Eknath Shinde is well aware of the situation, as well as how our alliance partner (BJP) treated Shiv Sena during the election,” he toldOn Gite’s sudden induction, Darade said he was informed by Shinde himself. “I was in Mumbai around 4pm on Monday when I received a call from deputy CM Shinde. He explained that it was crucial to strengthen the party’s presence in the legislative council and expand our footprint in Nashik district, which is why Gokul Gite was being brought in. I told him I had no objections,” he said.
Article Summary: Independent candidate Gokul Gite defeated Shiv Sena’s official candidate Narendra Darade in the Nashik local authorities election. Gite secured 357 votes in the election, defeating Darade by 109 votes and comfortably crossing the winning quota of 303.

Article Title: Three social welfare dept employees caught in bribery trap
Article Text:
Three social welfare dept employees caught in bribery trap





Chennai: Three employees of the social welfare department attached to the Thiruporur panchayat development office (PDO) in Chengalpet district were caught by officials of the Directorate of Vigilance and Anti-Corruption (DVAC) while accepting a bribe of ₹1,000 from a woman who approached them seeking welfare assistance for her two daughters.According to DVAC officials, Sathya Priya, 30, of Karumbakkam village in Thiruporur taluk, applied for welfare assistance for her daughters through the social welfare department section of the Thiruporur PDO office.While processing her application, Jayalakshmi , 57, an assistant in the department, demanded ₹1,000 to facilitate the sanction of the welfare benefits. Unwilling to pay the bribe, Sathya Priya approached the Chengalpet DVAC and lodged a complaint.Based on the complaint, DVAC officials arranged a trap and instructed the complainant to hand over the bribe as demanded. On the scheduled day, when Sathya Priya visited the office, Jayalakshmi directed Sathya Priya to hand over the money to Usharani, 57, the union social welfare officer. The cash was subsequently received by another assistant, Valarmathi, 52.DVAC personnel, led by Inspector Thiruvengadam, who were monitoring the transaction, intervened and caught the three women employees immediately after the money changed hands. All of them were taken into custody and questioned. A case was registered.
Article Summary: Sathya Priya, 30, of Karumbakkam village in Thiruporur taluk, applied for welfare assistance for her daughters. While processing her application, Jayalakshmi, 57, an assistant in the department, demanded ₹1,000 to facilitate the sanction of the welfare benefits. Unwilling to pay the bribe, Sathya. Priya approached the Chengalpet DVAC and lodged a complaint.

Article Title: Farmer dies of snakebite near Erode
Article Text:
Erode





: A 65-year-old farmer died after being bitten by a snake while he was working in his agricultural field in J Chettipalli village in Erode district on Tuesday morning.Police said the Naga Naikkar was engaged in agricultural work around 6.45am when the snake bit him. “Hearing his cries, nearby villagers rushed to him and took him to the Thalavadi govt hospital,” said K Palani, a villager. Later, he was referred to Chamrajnagar Govt Hospital, where he died.Naga Naikkar is survived by his wife Chinnammal.The Thalavadi police registered a case.
Article Summary: Naga Naikkar was engaged in agricultural work around 6.45am when the snake bit him. Villagers rushed to him and took him to the Thalavadi govt hospital. He was referred to Chamrajnagar Govt Hospital, where he died.

Article Title: Man says he was assaulted for erecting banner welcoming TVK minister to temple near Namakkal
Article Text:
N Sengamalai





NAMAKKAL: Congress party member N Sengamalai, 50, was allegedly assaulted by a man and his two sons on June 18 night for erecting a banner welcoming commercial tax and registration minister Lokesh Tamilselvan to the Dharmaraja temple at Thimmanaickenpatti in Namakkal district on June 14.Sengamalai, who is undergoing treatment at Salem Government Mohan Kumaramangalam Medical College and Hospital, alleged that Senthil and his two sons had told him that allowing a dalit minister into the temple was a “sin.”“They told me I had committed a crime by welcoming a dalit minister into the temple. For that alone, they beat me mercilessly,” Sengamalai told TOI. He added that he sustained bleeding injuries on his head, shoulder and legs.According to him, the trio, said to be DMK cadres, were preventing the Mangalapuram police from taking action against them. He alleged that they had threatened to murder him if he returned to the village after being discharged. Sengamalai appealed to the police and chief minister C Joseph Vijay to ensure his safety. Tamil Nadu Congress Committee Spokesperson Dr P V Senthil condemned the attack. “Every devotee has the right to worship, and every minister has the right to be welcomed. We appeal to the state govt to provide police protection to the victim and his family without delay,” he said.Namagiripettai block Congress president T P Elangovan said, “This is caste arrogance at its worst.”Senthil’s mobile phone was switched off when TOI tried to contact him.
Article Summary: Congress party member N Sengamalai, 50, was allegedly assaulted by a man and his two sons on June 18 night for erecting a banner welcoming commercial tax and registration minister Lokesh Tamilselvan to the Dharmaraja temple in Namakkal district.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.

Article Title: Madurai youth’s organs save four lives
Article Text:
Madurai

:





The organs of a 28-year-old youth who died following a road accident were donated, helping save the lives of four people.Muralikannan from Madurai district was riding a two-wheeler when he rammed into the Palanganatham bridge wall around 5.10pm on Monday. He suffered a severe head injury and was admitted to the Government Rajaji Hospital (GRH), Madurai, in an unconscious state. Despite treatment by the neurosurgery and intensive care teams, Muralikannan was declared brain dead at 5.15am on Wednesday. His parents consented to organ donation , following which the harvesting procedure was carried out at the trauma care centre.His liver was donated to Apollo Hospital, Madurai, while one kidney was transplanted at Government Mahatma Gandhi Memorial Hospital and the other at Dr Murugan Hospital in Trichy. His corneas were donated to GRH, Madurai.After postmortem examination at GRH, the body was handed over to the relatives through the police department. Hospital authorities paid homage to the deceased, while the Madurai district administration accorded state honours.
Article Summary: Muralikannan from Madurai district was riding a two-wheeler when he rammed into the Palanganatham bridge wall around 5.10pm on Monday. He suffered a severe head injury and was admitted to the Government Rajaji Hospital (GRH), Madurai, in an unconscious state. Despite treatment by the neurosurgery and intensive care teams, he was declared brain dead at 5.15am on Wednesday.

Article Title: Four arrested for robbing victims lured via dating app in Dindigul
Article Text:
Dindigul:





Four men were arrested by the Dindigul district police on Monday for targeting victims through a dating app and robbing them in isolated areas.According to police, the accused contacted individuals via the mobile application Grindr, which serves the LGBTQ community, and lured them to desolate locations before robbing them of valuables.In one instance, a man from Mettupatti was asked to meet near the Muthalagupatti junction on the Palani-Madurai road around 11.45pm on June 18. The gang allegedly assaulted him and fled with his two-wheeler worth Rs2 lakh and three mobile phones.In a similar incident on June 20, another man from Dindigul was reportedly in contact with the accused via the app, and under the pretext of having a meetup, the accused called the victim to a location, and the victim was robbed of two mobile phones.Based on complaints, Dindigul Taluk police registered two cases and launched a probe. Acting on the directions of superintendent of police S Jayakumar, a special team led by sub-inspector Arun Prasad tracked down and arrested the four accused — Naveenraja, 30; Kishore Velankanni, 26; Charles, alias Thulluvettiyan, 35; and Nova, 27.All stolen items were recovered and seized. The accused were produced before a court and remanded in judicial custody on Monday.
Article Summary: Four men were arrested by the Dindigul district police on Monday for targeting victims through a dating app and robbing them in isolated areas. According to police, the accused contacted individuals via the mobile application Grindr, which serves the LGBTQ community, and lured them to desolate locations.

Article Title: Produce medical records in Ramnad woman’s death case: HC to state
Article Text: Madras high court on Monday directed authorities to file a report and produce complete medical records in a case filed by the husband of a 35-year-old woman who died after undergoing treatment at Government Medical College Hospital, Ramanathapuram.
Article Summary: Madras high court on Monday directed authorities to file a report and produce complete medical records. The case was filed by the husband of a 35-year-old woman who died after undergoing treatment at Government Medical College Hospital, Ramanathapuram.
